sentence: Since I start efavirenz, skin rash dey disturb me plenty all over my body.
The rash started before I began efavirenz — it was already there when the doctor put me on the drug.
After starting nevirapine the rash developed on my chest and arms within the first two weeks.
I had that rash even before they put me on nevirapine; the doctor confirmed it was not related.
Patient reports persistent nausea that began shortly after initiating isoniazid therapy.
My mother get the nausea before dem start her on isoniazid — the doctors say na the ulcer cause am.
Since I begin metformin I dey vomit every morning — the nausea is too much.
The nausea was already there before I started metformin; my stomach has been troubling me for years.
After starting efavirenz, I have been feeling dizzy every morning when I wake up.
I had dizziness before I was put on efavirenz — it started when I had malaria last year.
Since I started amlodipine I feel light-headed every time I stand up quickly.
The dizziness started before I was given amlodipine; the doctor says it is low blood pressure that was there already.
Since starting pyrazinamide my joints dey pain me badly — I no fit walk well at all.
I had joint pain long before they started me on pyrazinamide — it is my old arthritis.
Since starting artesunate-amodiaquine my joints dey pain me badly — e dey hurt plenty.
The joint pain was there before I even took artesunate-amodiaquine; I have had it since childhood.
Since I started co-trimoxazole I have been running to the toilet every hour with watery stool.
The co-trimoxazole don cause diarrhea before — my child's stool was loose before dem even start the drug.
After starting amoxicillin the diarrhea started and has not stopped for three days now.
I had the loose stool before I was given amoxicillin; it began after I ate bad food at the chop bar.
Since starting tenofovir, I feel so tired I cannot do any work at all.
The fatigue was already there before I started tenofovir — I had been anaemic for months.
After initiating rifampicin the patient developed extreme fatigue and weakness.
Patient's fatigue predates rifampicin therapy — it was documented at the initial clinic visit before treatment began.
Since I started mefloquine I get terrible headaches every day; it is disturbing me too much.
I had the headache even before they gave me mefloquine — it has been my problem since secondary school.
After taking doxycycline the headache started and has been persistent since then.
The headache started before I began doxycycline; it was brought on by the stress at work.
Since I started ethambutol I have noticed my vision is blurring, especially when I read.
My father had vision problems before ethambutol was started — he has had cataracts for a long time.
After starting efavirenz I am seeing strange colours at night and my vision is not clear.
The vision changes were there before efavirenz — the optician found it when I went for glasses.
Since starting artesunate-amodiaquine my child says sounds are muffled and she cannot hear properly.
My child had the hearing problem before we gave her artesunate-amodiaquine — the ENT doctor saw it two months ago.
After starting lisinopril I have been coughing every night — a dry cough that will not stop.
I had the cough before I started lisinopril; I am an asthmatic and it is my usual cough.
Since I started amlodipine my feet have been swelling — the ankles are very big by evening.
The swelling in my legs started before amlodipine — it is because of my kidney problem that was there before.
After starting isoniazid I feel tingling in my hands and feet — like pins and needles all the time.
I had the tingling sensations in my feet before isoniazid was started; I have diabetic neuropathy already.
I dey get bad dreams and I no fit sleep since I start efavirenz last month — e dey disturb me every night.
I had nightmares two years ago during a very stressful period but they went away on their own before I started efavirenz.
Since starting nevirapine last month the itching all over my body has been constant.
I had that itching problem years ago — it resolved before I ever started nevirapine and has nothing to do with my current medication.
My child has been getting a rash every week since we started co-trimoxazole three weeks ago.
My child had a similar rash two years ago — it cleared up long before co-trimoxazole was even started; the doctors said it was eczema.
I dey feel dizzy and I dey vomit since I start artemether-lumefantrine for this malaria — e still dey happen.
The dizziness was something I had years ago — it stopped long before they gave me artemether-lumefantrine this time.
Since I start metformin last month I dey get loose stool every morning — the problem still dey there.
I had diarrhea two years ago because of a stomach infection, but it cleared on its own well before metformin was introduced.
I am currently experiencing numbness in my toes and fingers since starting isoniazid six weeks ago.
Patient experienced numbness in the extremities three years ago following a road traffic accident — this resolved before isoniazid was initiated.
Since starting rifampicin my urine has been orange-red — it is happening every day.
Patient reported orange discolouration of urine historically, two years ago, attributed to a dietary supplement — this was before rifampicin was started and is unrelated.
I have been getting heartburn and acid feeling in my chest since I started doxycycline last week.
I had heartburn badly several years ago but it went away on its own before doxycycline was started; my stomach has been fine until now.
My joints have been swollen and painful since I started pyrazinamide — the pain is there every day.
The joint swelling happened once before, years ago, and resolved completely before pyrazinamide was ever prescribed.
I have had a persistent dry cough every single night since starting lisinopril four weeks ago.
I had a cough like this years ago when I had TB — that was fully treated and gone before I started lisinopril.
Since starting abacavir I have been feeling feverish, tired, and getting a rash — all within the past month.
She had fever and rash two years ago during a malaria episode — this fully resolved before abacavir was started and the doctors say it is different.
I dey get headache almost every day since I start lamivudine last month — e no go away.
Patient experienced chronic daily headaches three years ago which resolved spontaneously well before lamivudine was initiated.
Since starting mefloquine I have been anxious and unable to sleep — it is happening every night.
The sleep difficulties were a problem years ago during a bereavement but they resolved before I was put on mefloquine.
My vision has been blurring progressively since I started ethambutol six weeks ago.
Patient had blurring of vision years ago due to a refractive error — corrected with glasses before ethambutol was started.
I have been having tinnitus — a ringing in my ears — since I started taking aspirin daily.
I had ringing in my ears years ago after a loud concert but it resolved well before I started aspirin and is not related.
Since I started ibuprofen for my back pain my stomach has been very painful every time I eat.
I had stomach ulcer pains years ago — treated and healed long before I started ibuprofen this time.
Since starting amoxicillin my child has been vomiting and having stomach pain every day.
The child had vomiting episodes two years ago from a rotavirus infection — resolved fully before amoxicillin was ever given.
I have been feeling my muscles are weak and my legs ache since I started tenofovir last month.
The muscle weakness occurred years ago when I had a bad flu — it resolved before tenofovir was started.
Since starting artesunate-amodiaquine I have been feeling very weak and cannot eat well.
Patient was very weak and anorexic two years ago during a typhoid episode — this resolved before artesunate-amodiaquine was prescribed.
Since I started taking high-dose paracetamol for my pain I have been feeling nauseated constantly.
I had nausea and vomiting years ago during pregnancy — that ended before I started paracetamol and is completely unrelated.
I stop the nevirapine and within three days the rash just disappear completely — e comot.
I stopped the nevirapine but the rash did not change at all — it is still there just as it was before.
When we discontinued efavirenz, the nightmares and dizziness resolved completely within one week.
Even after stopping efavirenz the dizziness and bad dreams continued just the same — no change at all.
I stopped lisinopril and the cough disappeared after just a few days — I can sleep well now.
I stopped lisinopril as the doctor advised but the cough has not improved at all — it is still the same.
When the doctor stopped amlodipine the ankle swelling went down within a week.
Even after stopping amlodipine the swelling in my ankles continued and did not reduce.
We stopped isoniazid and the tingling in his hands resolved completely over the following two weeks.
We stopped isoniazid but the numbness and tingling in his hands did not get better at all.
After we stopped co-trimoxazole the rash faded completely within four days.
After stopping co-trimoxazole the rash remained unchanged — no sign of fading at all.
I stopped metformin and the diarrhea resolved within two days — my stomach is normal again.
I stopped metformin as the doctor said but the loose stool continued just the same.
When we discontinued pyrazinamide, the joint pain resolved and the patient could walk normally again.
Pyrazinamide was discontinued but the joint pain persisted without any improvement.
I stopped artemether-lumefantrine and the nausea and vomiting stopped completely within two days.
Even after stopping artemether-lumefantrine the nausea continued and I was still vomiting.
When doxycycline was stopped, the heartburn and chest discomfort resolved within three days.
Even after dem stop doxycycline the heartburn continue — no change at all.
We stopped rifampicin temporarily and the patient's itching resolved within five days.
Rifampicin was stopped but the itching did not reduce at all — the patient was still scratching.
When I stopped mefloquine, the anxiety and sleep problems resolved within one week.
I stopped mefloquine but the anxiety and insomnia continued — no change was noticed.
After stopping ethambutol, the patient's vision improved noticeably over two weeks.
Ethambutol was stopped but the patient's vision did not improve — it remained blurred as before.
I stopped ibuprofen and the stomach pain went away immediately — I feel fine now.
Even after I stopped ibuprofen the stomach pain continued — it did not improve at all.
When abacavir was discontinued the fever, rash, and fatigue resolved completely within a week.
Abacavir was discontinued but the fever and rash continued without any improvement.
I stopped the aspirin and the ringing in my ears stopped within a few days.
I stopped aspirin but the tinnitus continued just as loudly as before — no improvement.
When we stop amoxicillin my child's vomiting and stomach pain just comot within two days — she fine.
We stopped amoxicillin but the child's vomiting and stomach pain continued unchanged.
After stopping tenofovir the patient's fatigue and muscle weakness improved significantly over two weeks.
Tenofovir was stopped but the fatigue and weakness did not improve — the patient remained the same.
I stopped lamivudine and my headaches went away within a few days.
I stopped lamivudine as instructed but the headaches continued every day — no improvement.
When artesunate-amodiaquine was discontinued, the patient's weakness and poor appetite resolved within a week.
We stop artesunate-amodiaquine but the weakness and poor appetite no change — patient still dey suffer.
When I restarted nevirapine the rash came back immediately — within two days it was all over my body.
I took nevirapine again and had no rash at all this time — no reaction, nothing.
We rechallenged with efavirenz and the same dizziness and nightmares occurred again within three days.
Patient was rechallenged with efavirenz and had no dizziness or nightmares — completely tolerating the drug.
When isoniazid was restarted the tingling in his hands returned within one week.
Isoniazid was restarted and the patient had no tingling or numbness — no adverse reaction.
We rechallenged with co-trimoxazole and the rash appeared again on the second day.
Co-trimoxazole was restarted and no rash developed — the patient tolerated it without any problem.
When I started lisinopril again the cough came back within four days.
I started lisinopril again and this time I had no cough at all — the doctor says I am tolerating it fine.
When dem restart amlodipine the ankle swelling come back — the feet big again within two weeks.
Amlodipine was reintroduced and there was no swelling — the patient had no discomfort at all.
I started metformin again and the diarrhea returned immediately — worse than before.
I took metformin again and had no diarrhea this time — my stomach was fine throughout.
When doxycycline was restarted the heartburn returned on the very first day.
We rechallenged with doxycycline and the patient reported no heartburn or discomfort.
When I took artemether-lumefantrine again the nausea and vomiting returned just as before.
I took artemether-lumefantrine again for a new malaria episode — no nausea, no vomiting, I was fine.
We rechallenged with abacavir and the same hypersensitivity reaction — fever, rash, and fatigue — occurred again.
Abacavir was rechallenged with careful monitoring and the patient had no fever, no rash, no reaction.
When mefloquine was restarted the anxiety and bad dreams came back the very first week.
She get mefloquine again and no anxiety, no bad dream — she dey tolerate am fine.
When rifampicin was reintroduced the patient developed itching again within five days.
Rifampicin was reintroduced and the patient had no itching — she tolerated it without any complaint.
We rechallenged with pyrazinamide and the joint pain recurred just as severely as before.
Pyrazinamide was rechallenged and no joint pain occurred — the patient is tolerating it well.
When ethambutol was restarted the blurred vision returned within two weeks.
Ethambutol was restarted and the patient reported no vision problems — tolerating the drug without issue.
When I took ibuprofen again the stomach pain came back immediately — I had to stop again.
I took ibuprofen again and my stomach was completely fine — no pain, no discomfort.
When I restarted aspirin the tinnitus came back within a few days.
Aspirin was restarted and the ringing in the ears did not return — no reaction was noticed.
When we gave the child amoxicillin again the vomiting and stomach pain returned on the second day.
We rechallenged the child with amoxicillin and she had no vomiting or stomach pain — she tolerated it fine.
When tenofovir was reintroduced the patient's fatigue and muscle weakness returned within two weeks.
We restart tenofovir and the patient dey fine — no fatigue, no muscle ache at all.
When I restarted lamivudine the headaches came back within a few days.
I restarted lamivudine and had no headaches at all this time — I am fine.
We rechallenged with artesunate-amodiaquine and the same weakness and anorexia occurred again.
Artesunate-amodiaquine was rechallenged and the patient had no weakness or anorexia — no reaction.
I am still getting dizziness every morning since I started efavirenz — it has not improved at all.
The dizziness I had from efavirenz cleared up completely after the second week. I am fine now.
The vivid dreams from efavirenz are ongoing and disrupting my sleep every single night.
Those vivid dreams from efavirenz settled down on their own — I sleep without any problem now.
A rash appeared on my chest and arms after I began nevirapine and it is still spreading.
The rash I had when I started nevirapine has completely resolved. No more skin problem.
I keep having nausea every time I take nevirapine — it will not go away no matter what I eat.
I had some nausea at first on nevirapine but it settled after the first week and I feel better now.
My joint pain has been persistent since I started isoniazid; both knees ache daily.
The joint pain from isoniazid has gone — it passed about three weeks into treatment.
I am experiencing tingling in my hands continuously since beginning isoniazid treatment.
The tingling in my hands that started with isoniazid has resolved completely with the vitamin B6 supplement.
Stomach upset is ongoing since I started rifampicin — I feel sick after every dose.
The stomach upset I had on rifampicin has cleared up. I take it with food now and I am fine.
I feel extreme fatigue every day and it has not lifted since beginning rifampicin.
That fatigue from rifampicin passed after about ten days. I have my energy back now.
The headache started when I took artemether-lumefantrine and it is still with me three days on.
The headache from artemether-lumefantrine went away by the next morning — no longer bothering me.
I am still feeling dizzy after finishing the artemether-lumefantrine course; it won't stop.
The rash don clear — dizziness from the artemether-lumefantrine left after I completed the course. I dey fine now.
Diarrhea is persisting since I started metformin — I am going to the toilet many times a day.
The diarrhea I had when metformin was first started has resolved. Stool is now normal.
Nausea after each metformin dose is still a problem — I cannot eat properly.
I had nausea from metformin initially but it has settled down. No more problem now.
My ankles have been swelling continuously since the doctor put me on amlodipine.
The swelling in my ankles from amlodipine has completely gone — the doctor adjusted the dose and it resolved.
I am having a headache every day since starting amlodipine; it is getting worse, not better.
The headache that came with amlodipine cleared up after the first few days. I no longer have that issue.
I have been itching all over my body since I started co-trimoxazole and I cannot sleep from it.
The itching from co-trimoxazole passed once the course ended. It has not returned.
A rash appeared on my back after starting co-trimoxazole and is still present and spreading.
That rash from co-trimoxazole has cleared up — the doctor applied some cream and it is better now.
Every time I swallow the doxycycline tablet I get nausea that lasts for hours — still happening.
I had nausea with doxycycline at first but it has completely gone. I take it with a full glass of water now.
Stomach upset from doxycycline is still ongoing — sharp pain in my upper abdomen after each dose.
The stomach upset from doxycycline settled down by day four. No more issue with it.
I developed a rash all over after starting amoxicillin and it is still worsening.
The rash I got from amoxicillin has resolved — it faded within days of stopping the medication.
Diarrhea is still ongoing since the amoxicillin was started — very loose stools multiple times daily.
Diarrhea from amoxicillin has cleared. Stool returned to normal consistency three days after finishing the course.
I am feeling fatigued every day since being switched to tenofovir — I can barely walk to the clinic.
The fatigue that started when I was put on tenofovir has since resolved. I feel stronger now.
Muscle pain is ongoing and has not improved since I started tenofovir three weeks ago.
The muscle pain from tenofovir has gone away on its own — I had it for about a week then it stopped.
Joint pain in my shoulders and hips is still ongoing since pyrazinamide was added to my regimen.
The joint pain from pyrazinamide has resolved after uric acid treatment was added. I dey fine now.
Nausea and vomiting persist — every dose of pyrazinamide makes me feel sick and it is still happening.
That nausea from pyrazinamide settled after the first ten days. Patient reports no further discomfort.
I have been dizzy and unsteady on my feet since taking mefloquine — still the same today.
The dizziness from mefloquine passed two days after the dose. I am no longer experiencing it.
A pounding headache has been with me since I took mefloquine for prophylaxis and won't go away.
The headache that came after mefloquine cleared up by day three. No longer a problem.
I still have a rash on my arms and face three weeks after starting efavirenz — it is not going away.
The rash from efavirenz cleared up completely. Skin is back to normal and no longer any itching.
Fatigue is still severe — every day since starting isoniazid I feel too weak to do anything.
I had fatigue from isoniazid at first but it has now resolved. I am managing fine on the treatment.
I have been itching all over since starting rifampicin — it is still there every day after I take it.
The itching from rifampicin cleared after the first two weeks. It has not returned since.
Nausea from co-trimoxazole is an ongoing issue — I feel queasy for hours after every dose.
I had nausea from co-trimoxazole at the start but it has settled down. No more nausea at all.
I believe the stomach cramps I am having are from the metformin — they started the very day I began taking it.
The stomach cramps are not from metformin — the doctor confirmed they are due to a GI infection, unrelated to my medication.
The cough started right after I was put on amlodipine and the doctor thinks the amlodipine is likely causing it.
The cough is not from amlodipine — tests confirmed it is a respiratory infection and has nothing to do with my blood pressure medication.
I have been feeling low and depressed since starting efavirenz — the counsellor suspects the drug is the cause.
The depression is not from efavirenz — psychiatric review confirmed it is unrelated to the antiretroviral and has a separate cause.
I am having pain in my right side and the doctor suspects the isoniazid is affecting my liver.
The liver pain is not caused by isoniazid — liver biopsy showed another aetiology, not drug-induced.
The fever started right after I began co-trimoxazole — I think the drug is the one causing it.
The fever is not from co-trimoxazole — the doctor confirmed it is a separate opportunistic infection, not a drug reaction.
The rash appeared within 24 hours of starting doxycycline. The doctor believes doxycycline is responsible.
Tests showed the rash was not caused by doxycycline — it was an existing allergic reaction to another substance.
My vision has been blurring since rifampicin was started — I believe the medication is affecting my eyesight.
The vision changes are not from rifampicin — the ophthalmologist confirmed the cause is diabetic retinopathy, not the TB drug.
I am having pain in my lower back and the nephrologist suspects tenofovir nephrotoxicity is the cause.
The kidney pain is not from tenofovir — urology review found a kidney stone that is the real cause, not the medication.
The vomiting started immediately after the first artemether-lumefantrine dose — the nurse thinks the drug caused it.
The vomiting is not from artemether-lumefantrine — the doctor confirmed it is from gastroenteritis that started before the drug was given.
My face swelled up after the first amoxicillin tablet — the doctor says it is an allergic reaction to the drug.
The swelling of the face is not from amoxicillin — allergy testing confirmed the trigger was a food allergen taken at the same time, not the antibiotic.
I am not sure if it is the efavirenz, but since I started taking it I have been having very vivid nightmares and I wake up feeling completely disoriented.
I am not sure if it is the isoniazid, but since I started taking it my joints have been aching terribly, especially in the mornings when I try to get up.
Patient uncertain whether rash is related to rifampicin, but temporal association noted — rash appeared within four days of treatment initiation and has persisted.
I am not sure if it is the metformin, but ever since I began the tablets I have had constant stomach pain and loose stools that make it hard to go to work.
I am not sure if it is the artemether-lumefantrine, but since my daughter started the course she has been vomiting after every dose and she is too weak to eat.
I no sure if na the amlodipine, but since I start take am my feet dey swell plenty and I dey feel dizzy when I stand up fast.
I am not sure if it is the nevirapine, but since starting the regimen two weeks ago I have had a rash on my chest that is spreading and itching without stopping.
Patient uncertain whether fatigue is attributable to tenofovir, but reports onset coincides with initiation of regimen and has worsened progressively over three weeks.
I am not sure if it is the doxycycline, but since I started the tablets I have been getting severe headaches every day that do not go away even with paracetamol.
I am not sure if it is the co-trimoxazole, but since we started giving my son the syrup he has been breaking out in a rash all over his body and crying from the itching.
I think it might be from the pyrazinamide — I have been having severe joint pain in both knees every day and it only started after we added that tablet to my treatment.
Patient thinks the persistent dry cough might be from lisinopril. Cough has been present daily since antihypertensive was started six weeks ago.
I think it might be the efavirenz — I have been having strange mood changes and feeling very low almost every afternoon, and it only began after I started the ARVs.
I think it might be the amoxicillin — my child has been having diarrhoea every single day since we started the course and the stool is sometimes mixed with mucus.
I think maybe the isoniazid be the thing giving me this problem. Since I start take am, I dey feel like the ground dey spin and I fall small-small sometimes.
Patient suspects metformin may be causing nausea. Reports daily nausea with each dose since initiating therapy; no prior gastrointestinal history.
I think it might be from the rifampicin — I have been having orange-coloured urine and my skin feels itchy all over, and it has been like this since the first week of treatment.
I think the amlodipine might be causing my severe headaches — I get them almost every morning now and they were not there before I started the blood pressure medicine.
Could the nevirapine be causing this rash? It started exactly when I began the treatment and it keeps getting worse no matter what cream I apply.
Could tenofovir be responsible for the muscle pain? Patient reports bilateral leg weakness and pain beginning within one week of starting the regimen.
Could the co-trimoxazole be causing my nausea? It started right when I began the prophylaxis and I feel like vomiting every time I swallow the tablet.
Could the artemether-lumefantrine be causing my child to have such bad dizziness? He keeps falling and says everything looks like it is spinning, and it started on the first day of the treatment.
Could doxycycline be causing my vision to blur? It started right when I began the course and I am struggling to read texts on my phone at night.
Is it possible the lisinopril is giving me this dry cough? The cough started immediately after I started taking the tablet and it has not gone since then.
Could the amoxicillin be causing my daughter's rash? The bumps appeared on her second day of the course and they are still spreading across her stomach and back.
Could the pyrazinamide be what is making my stomach hurt so badly? The pain started right after they added it to my TB treatment and it is there every single day.
I thought it was just stress from work, but every single time I take the efavirenz at night I have nightmares and wake up in a cold sweat — it happens without fail.
Patient initially attributed tingling in the hands to heavy lifting at work. However, the temporal pattern is consistent with isoniazid-induced peripheral neuropathy following six weeks of treatment.
I thought the nausea was from something I ate, but every time I take the rifampicin in the morning I feel sick for the next two or three hours — it happens every day.
I thought my son was just weak from the malaria itself, but every time he takes the artemether-lumefantrine tablet he vomits within thirty minutes and then becomes very sleepy.
I think say e be the food I chop, but every time I take the metformin my belly dey pain me well well and I go toilet plenty. E happen every time.
I thought the swelling in my ankles was from being on my feet all day, but it has been there every morning since I started the amlodipine — even when I rest.
I thought the headache was from the sun, but I get a pounding headache every day after taking the doxycycline and it lasts for hours — it never happened before the antibiotics.
I assumed my baby was just teething, but every time I give the co-trimoxazole drops she develops a rash around her mouth within the hour and it fades overnight.
It might be nothing, but since the nevirapine was added to my regimen I have had a rash on my arms and chest that will not go away no matter what I do.
Patient minimises symptoms but has had persistent fatigue and bone pain since tenofovir was added to the antiretroviral regimen eight weeks ago.
It may be nothing serious, but since pyrazinamide was added to my treatment I have had joint pain in my fingers and wrists every morning that makes it hard to grip things.
It might not be a big problem, but since my child started the amoxicillin she has had loose stools three or four times a day that will not stop, and she is losing weight.
Maybe I am just overthinking it, but since they put me on lisinopril for my pressure I have this dry cough that has not stopped for even one day since I started.
It might be nothing, but since the metformin was added to my diabetes treatment I have had constant nausea and stomach cramps after every meal that were not there before.
It might not be related, but since I started the doxycycline I have been having severe sun sensitivity — my skin burns and blisters even after a short time outside.
Patient dismisses itching as minor but reports it has been present daily since rifampicin was initiated and is now disturbing sleep; no prior dermatological history.
I am on three medications — I do not know which one is causing it, but I have been having severe dizziness and strange dreams every night since they started efavirenz last month.
Patient on multiple TB drugs and uncertain which agent is responsible, but developed peripheral tingling and numbness shortly after isoniazid was added to the regimen.
I take four different tablets and I do not know which one is making me feel so tired all the time, but the fatigue got much worse after they introduced tenofovir to my regimen.
My daughter is on multiple medicines and I cannot tell which is causing the rash, but it appeared the same week they added co-trimoxazole and it has been spreading every day.
I dey take plenty medicine so I no know which one dey cause am, but since dem add the amlodipine my legs dey swell and I dey feel pain for my chest small-small.
They have me on several medications and I honestly do not know which one is responsible, but I have been having nausea and stomach cramps since they started rifampicin three weeks ago.
My husband is on multiple TB tablets and we are not sure which one is causing the joint pain, but it started when they added pyrazinamide to his treatment and he can barely walk now.
Patient unable to identify causative agent given polypharmacy, but hepatotoxicity markers elevated and temporal association with nevirapine introduction is clinically significant.
ALT 156 U/L (3.2× ULN) noted on day 28 of isoniazid therapy — drug-induced liver injury suspected, isoniazid held pending review.
Liver function check shows ALT 42 U/L (1.1× ULN) at week 4 of isoniazid — mildly elevated but sub-threshold, continue current regimen with monthly monitoring.
Transaminase markedly raised: ALT 320 U/L (8.0× ULN) after 6 weeks on rifampicin-containing regimen — hepatotoxicity confirmed, regimen suspended.
ALT 58 U/L (1.5× ULN) on routine LFT while on rifampicin — transaminase elevation present but not clinically significant; monitoring continued.
Patient on pyrazinamide reports right upper quadrant discomfort; ALT 210 U/L (5.4× ULN) — drug-induced hepatitis, pyrazinamide withdrawn immediately.
Liver enzymes checked at 8-week visit: ALT 72 U/L (1.9× ULN) while on pyrazinamide — mild elevation below 3× ULN threshold, no dose adjustment at this time.
ALT 185 U/L (4.7× ULN) detected on week 6 of nevirapine-based ART — hepatotoxicity reaction, nevirapine discontinued and substitution initiated.
Routine LFT on nevirapine shows ALT 88 U/L (2.3× ULN) — transaminase 2.3 times upper limit of normal; sub-threshold, patient counselled and follow-up in 2 weeks.
ALT 148 U/L (3.8× ULN) recorded at month 3 on efavirenz — clinically significant liver enzyme elevation meeting DILI criteria, regimen review initiated.
LFT results: ALT 65 U/L (1.7× ULN) on efavirenz at 12-week check — mild transaminase rise, below the 3× ULN action threshold, continue with monitoring.
Follow-up LFT shows ALT 245 U/L (6.3× ULN) after 16 weeks of methotrexate — hepatotoxicity suspected, drug held and gastroenterology referral placed.
ALT 95 U/L (2.4× ULN) on monthly monitoring during methotrexate therapy — elevation noted but does not reach 3× ULN threshold; liver biopsy deferred, close follow-up arranged.
Week 12 LFT: ALT 162 U/L (4.2× ULN) in patient on isoniazid and pyridoxine — values exceed 3× ULN, drug-induced liver injury protocol activated.
ALT 50 U/L (1.3× ULN) at routine hepatic monitoring while on rifampicin — transaminase at 1.3 times upper limit; within acceptable watch-and-wait range, continue therapy.
Severe hepatotoxicity on nevirapine: ALT 510 U/L (13.1× ULN), patient jaundiced — treatment immediately stopped, supportive care commenced.
ALT measured at 80 U/L (2.1× ULN) during efavirenz monitoring at month 2 — physician notes sub-threshold elevation, no clinical action warranted at present.
Liver panel shows ALT 118 U/L (3.0× ULN) exactly at the threshold after 10 weeks of pyrazinamide — classified as clinically significant DILI; drug withheld.
ALT 78 U/L (2.0× ULN) on methotrexate 15 mg weekly — not yet at action threshold; folic acid supplementation confirmed and monitoring frequency increased.
Patient on isoniazid presents with fatigue and nausea; ALT 290 U/L (7.4× ULN) — hepatotoxicity adverse event confirmed, drug stopped and notified to pharmacovigilance unit.
LFT at 4-week mark on rifampicin: ALT 110 U/L (2.8× ULN) — elevated but remains below 3× ULN clinical action threshold; intensify monitoring to biweekly.
Serum creatinine doubled from baseline 98 μmol/L to 202 μmol/L (2.1× baseline) after 8 weeks on tenofovir — nephrotoxicity confirmed, drug substituted with abacavir.
Creatinine increased to 128 μmol/L from baseline 105 μmol/L (1.2× baseline) on tenofovir — mildly elevated within acceptable range, no dose adjustment required at this point.
Day 7 of gentamicin therapy: creatinine 260 μmol/L, up from baseline 118 μmol/L (2.2× baseline) — acute kidney injury attributed to aminoglycoside nephrotoxicity, drug discontinued.
Renal function check on day 4 of gentamicin: creatinine 155 μmol/L from baseline 120 μmol/L (1.3× baseline) — sub-threshold rise, ensure adequate hydration and continue monitoring.
Creatinine rose to 310 μmol/L from pre-treatment baseline of 90 μmol/L (3.4× baseline) after amphotericin B infusions — severe nephrotoxicity, liposomal formulation substituted.
Renal profile after 5 days of amphotericin B: creatinine 148 μmol/L vs baseline 110 μmol/L (1.35× baseline) — mild creatinine rise not exceeding 2× baseline, saline pre-hydration reinforced.
Post-cycle creatinine 240 μmol/L compared to pre-cisplatin baseline of 88 μmol/L (2.7× baseline) — nephrotoxicity event documented, dose reduction and nephrology review requested.
Creatinine 132 μmol/L from baseline 95 μmol/L (1.4× baseline) on day 10 post-cisplatin — kidney function mildly impacted but below 2× baseline action threshold, aggressive hydration maintained.
eGFR declined to 52 mL/min/1.73m² (below 60 threshold) and creatinine 218 μmol/L on tenofovir at 6-month review — tenofovir nephrotoxicity, regimen switched.
Creatinine 170 μmol/L from baseline 125 μmol/L (1.36× baseline) after 6 days gentamicin — renal function mildly affected; sub-threshold, trough levels checked and dose interval extended.
Acute kidney injury on amphotericin B: serum creatinine 350 μmol/L from baseline 100 μmol/L (3.5× baseline), eGFR 18 mL/min — drug held, urgent nephrology consulted.
Creatinine 140 μmol/L vs pre-treatment 102 μmol/L (1.37× baseline) on day 7 post-cisplatin cycle — within acceptable range (below 2× baseline), continue current hydration protocol.
Creatinine doubled to 198 μmol/L from baseline 99 μmol/L (2.0× baseline) — nephrotoxicity threshold reached on tenofovir; regimen change to abacavir-based ART initiated.
Day 5 renal monitoring on gentamicin: creatinine 158 μmol/L from baseline 118 μmol/L (1.34× baseline) — sub-threshold elevation, no adjustment yet; recheck in 48 hours.
Post-chemotherapy review: creatinine 226 μmol/L from cisplatin pre-treatment baseline of 92 μmol/L (2.5× baseline) — significant nephrotoxicity, oncology team alerted and hydration escalated.
Creatinine 138 μmol/L from baseline 102 μmol/L (1.35× baseline) after 3 days amphotericin B — minor renal stress below nephrotoxicity threshold, oral hydration reinforced.
Platelet count dropped to 68,000/μL during co-trimoxazole therapy — thrombocytopenia adverse drug reaction, drug held and haematology notified.
Full blood count: platelets 125,000/μL on co-trimoxazole — borderline low but above the 100,000/μL clinical action threshold; weekly monitoring instituted.
Platelet count 45,000/μL in patient on quinine for malaria — severe thrombocytopenia attributed to quinine, drug discontinued and platelet transfusion considered.
Repeat CBC shows platelets 138,000/μL on day 5 of quinine — above the 100,000/μL threshold; platelets trending toward low-normal, continue quinine and recheck in 48 hours.
Week-3 CBC on linezolid: platelet count 72,000/μL — thrombocytopenia below critical threshold, linezolid-induced myelosuppression suspected, dose held.
Platelet count 112,000/μL at week 2 of linezolid — borderline low but above the 100,000/μL action threshold; haematological monitoring intensified to twice weekly.
CBC on valproate shows platelet count of 88,000/μL — clinically significant thrombocytopenia, dose reduction initiated and neurologist informed.
Routine blood count: platelets 143,000/μL while on valproate sodium — below normal range but above clinical action threshold of 100,000/μL; no dose change, monitor monthly.
Platelet count fell to 55,000/μL on day 14 of co-trimoxazole prophylaxis — meets thrombocytopenia criteria, drug withheld and dapsone substitution considered.
Platelets 130,000/μL at week-4 monitoring on linezolid — above the 100,000/μL threshold but trending down from 165,000/μL at baseline; close haematological follow-up maintained.
Platelet count 22,000/μL on day 7 of quinine — acute immune thrombocytopenia consistent with quinine-induced reaction, immediately discontinued.
Haematology panel: platelets 119,000/μL after 6 weeks on valproate — low-normal, currently above the 100,000/μL action threshold; repeat CBC in 4 weeks.
ECG performed after chloroquine loading dose shows QTc interval of 530ms — clinically significant QT prolongation exceeding 500ms threshold, chloroquine withheld.
ECG review on chloroquine: QTc 472ms — borderline prolongation noted, below the 500ms action threshold; cardiology advised and telemetry monitoring initiated.
Post-dose ECG on azithromycin shows QTc 518ms — QT interval prolongation above 500ms threshold, drug substituted with doxycycline after senior review.
Cardiac monitor: QTc 462ms during azithromycin course — prolonged relative to baseline 430ms but below the 500ms safety cutoff; continue with daily ECG surveillance.
ECG on haloperidol decanoate: QTc interval 508ms — exceeds the 500ms clinical threshold for significant QTc prolongation, psychiatry and cardiology consulted for dose review.
Routine ECG shows QTc 488ms on haloperidol 5 mg daily — borderline prolongation within watchful range (below 500ms), electrolytes corrected and ECG repeated in one week.
ECG performed during methadone maintenance: QTc 545ms — dangerous QT prolongation far exceeding 500ms, dose immediately reduced and cardiology review arranged.
Cardiac assessment on methadone 60 mg: QTc 490ms — QTc borderline elevated, within watchful range below 500ms threshold; continue methadone with bi-weekly ECG and electrolyte checks.
ECG check after 3 months on hydroxychloroquine: QTc interval 522ms — significant QTc prolongation exceeding 500ms, drug paused and alternative antimalarial prophylaxis considered.
ECG on hydroxychloroquine at 6-week visit: QTc 455ms — mildly prolonged from baseline 420ms but well below the 500ms action threshold; continue with monitoring.
Patient on chloroquine for malaria prophylaxis: repeat ECG shows QTc 560ms — critically prolonged QT interval, risk of torsades de pointes, chloroquine stopped immediately.

Follow-up ECG on day 3 of azithromycin: QTc 478ms — borderline prolongation, below the 500ms critical threshold; no action taken, patient reminded to avoid co-administration of other QT-prolonging agents.

I am on medication for my condition — I prefer not to say what it is in front of people — but since starting the new tablet, I have been hearing strange sounds and seeing things at night that are not there. It is very frightening and I cannot sleep properly.

My family does not know about my treatment and I take my tablets in private. But since I started them three weeks ago, I have been losing weight very fast and my hair is falling out in clumps. My clothes no longer fit me properly.

I do not discuss my treatment with anyone, not even my husband. Since I started the tablets my doctor gave me for my condition, I have been having very vivid and frightening dreams every night. I wake up sweating and confused about what is real.

I take it privately because people in my community will talk if they know. But since starting this medication, a very bad rash has appeared all over my chest and back and it is spreading. The itching is unbearable and keeps me awake.

I prefer not to say what I am being treated for — my neighbours must not know. Since they added this new tablet to my regimen, I have been feeling pins and needles in both feet that do not go away, even when I am resting. Walking has become very painful.

I am on medication for my condition but I cannot say what condition in front of my family. Since I began the treatment, I have been experiencing a constant dull pain in my right side below my ribs. The pain is worse after I take the tablet each evening.

My relatives do not know I am on treatment and I intend to keep it that way. But since starting this medicine, both my legs have been feeling very weak and I have numbness in my toes that travels up to my ankles. It is difficult to stand for long.

I take this treatment privately and I will not tell you the name of my condition. But since I started the new tablet about a month ago, I keep hearing voices calling my name at night when no one is there. My doctor said it is a known side effect but it is very disturbing.

People in my church do not know about my treatment and I am ashamed for them to find out. Since I began taking the tablets, my skin has been turning yellow and I feel very tired all the time. Even simple tasks at home leave me exhausted.

I keep my medication hidden in my room because my children must not know. Since I started this treatment, I have been having bad nightmares almost every night and I wake up very anxious. My mood has also changed — I feel very low and cry without reason.

I do not want my coworkers to know what I am being treated for, so I take my tablet alone at home at night. Since starting it, my dreams have become so vivid and strange — I see people and places that are frightening and very real. I am afraid to sleep now.

I prefer not to name my treatment here because my family is present. But since they changed my tablet three months ago, I have noticed my hair thinning badly and a rash on my neck that has not cleared. I have also lost about five kilograms without trying.

I was ashamed to tell my family I am on tablets for my mind, so I have kept this secret for two years. But since I started the white tablet, I have been having uncontrollable shaking in my hands and my mouth moves by itself. It is very embarrassing in public.

I do not want people to know I am on treatment for my nerves. Since starting the new medication, I cannot sleep at all and my heart is racing all the time even when I am just sitting quietly. I have also gained a lot of weight since I started.

I take these tablets quietly and I have not told my husband what they are for. Since I began them, my legs will not stay still — I feel compelled to keep moving and pacing, even at night. The restlessness is worse than the problem the medication is supposed to treat.

My family does not know I am receiving treatment for a mental condition and I prefer to keep it that way. Since they added this new tablet last month, my whole body has become very stiff and I move very slowly. I also feel like I am floating and not fully awake.

I am on tablets for my mind but I do not discuss this openly even with close friends. Since I began the treatment, I have been sleeping almost fourteen hours a day and I cannot function properly at work. My boss thinks I am lazy but it is the medication making me so drowsy.

I prefer not to say what I am treated for because people do not understand these conditions. But since I began the new tablet, my hands tremble constantly and I feel very thirsty all the time, drinking water all day. My urine has also increased greatly.

I take this privately — I am embarrassed for my community to know I am on psychiatric medication. Since starting it, I have gained almost ten kilograms in two months and my blood sugar was found to be high at my last check. These changes worry me very much.

I do not want my in-laws to know I am being treated for a condition of the mind. Since they started me on this new tablet, I have been losing a lot of hair and my weight has increased even though I have not changed what I eat. My hands also shake in the mornings.

I am on a program but I do not want to tell you what for — please understand. Since they started me on the new medication in the program, my stomach has been very bad every morning and I vomit before I can eat anything. This has been going on for three weeks now.

I attend a clinic for a personal matter I prefer not to discuss here. Since they changed my tablet last month, I have been severely constipated for days at a time and I sweat very heavily at night even though the room is not hot. My sleep is very poor.

My family does not know I am on treatment and I take my medication very privately. But since starting the program medication, I have been feeling very dizzy when I stand up quickly and twice I have nearly fallen. I also feel my heart beating strangely sometimes.

I do not discuss what I am being treated for — it is a private matter. Since I began taking the tablet I was prescribed, I have been having bad nausea every morning and I feel very tired throughout the day. I also get sudden headaches that come without warning.

I prefer not to say what my treatment is for, even to the nurse. Since they started me on the new tablet at the clinic, I sweat a lot even in cool conditions and my heart races in the afternoons. Some days I feel very anxious and shaky for no reason I can explain.

I am on hormone treatment but I do not discuss this openly with my family or community. Since starting, I have been having very bad headaches almost every day and my blood pressure was found to be high at the clinic. The doctor asked me to monitor it at home.

I take hormone medication privately and I do not want others to know about my treatment. Since I began, I have noticed swelling and pain in my left calf that has not gone away and the leg feels warm to touch. I am worried because it came on suddenly.

My treatment is a private matter and I prefer not to disclose it here. Since starting the hormone tablets, my mood has been changing very rapidly — I cry easily, then I feel angry, then very sad again within the same day. This did not happen before I started the medication.

I do not discuss my hormone treatment openly because people in Ghana do not understand. But since I started injections, I have been getting severe headaches that last for hours and my skin has broken out very badly on my face and back. This has not happened to me before.

I keep my hormone treatment private — even my closest relatives do not know. Since I began taking the tablets, I have been feeling tightness in my chest when I climb stairs and I get short of breath more easily than before. I also feel my heart pounding at times when I am resting.
We discontinued efavirenz and within three days the dizziness resolved completely. The patient had no further neurological complaints after stopping the drug.
The patient stopped taking co-trimoxazole and the rash disappeared within three days — positive dechallenge. This confirms the drug was responsible for the cutaneous reaction.
Since stopping isoniazid, the nausea has completely gone. This confirms drug-induced causation and the patient will not be rechallenged.
I stopped the nevirapine and within a week, the severe itching was fully gone. My skin is back to normal now.
Amoxicillin was discontinued and the urticarial rash resolved within 48 hours. Dechallenge was positive, confirming drug-induced urticaria.
We held rifampicin for ten days and the patient's liver pain resolved. Positive dechallenge strongly implicates rifampicin in the hepatotoxic reaction.
After I stopped taking doxycycline, the stomach cramps disappeared completely within two days. I am sure it was the tablets causing my pain.
Pyrazinamide was withdrawn and within one week the joint pain fully resolved — a clear positive dechallenge. The patient was then managed on a pyrazinamide-free regimen.
My doctor told me to stop the metformin and within three days my diarrhea was completely gone. I had been suffering for weeks and it was the drug all along.
Following discontinuation of artemether-lumefantrine, the patient reported complete resolution of headache and vomiting within 48 hours. This positive dechallenge response supports drug causality.
Since we stopped tenofovir, the fatigue has improved dramatically. The dechallenge is positive and the case is being reported to the national pharmacovigilance centre.
I stopped the chloroquine tablets and my dizziness went away after about four days. My mother also noticed I was walking better once I quit the medicine.
Amlodipine was discontinued and the patient's peripheral oedema and headache resolved within ten days. Positive dechallenge confirmed amlodipine-induced adverse effects.
We stopped co-trimoxazole prophylaxis and the nausea resolved within two days. Positive dechallenge; alternative prophylaxis was initiated.
The patient discontinued isoniazid on day 14 of treatment due to peripheral neuropathy. Within ten days of stopping, the tingling in the hands and feet resolved — dechallenge positive.
My wife stopped her efavirenz and within one week she stopped having those bad dreams and her dizziness was gone. The doctor said this was a positive dechallenge.
Drug withdrawn: nevirapine. Outcome: rash resolved within 5 days of discontinuation. Assessment: positive dechallenge; probable drug-induced Stevens-Johnson precursor reaction.
After stopping rifampicin, the patient's vomiting ceased within three days and liver enzymes began normalising. The positive dechallenge response confirms rifampicin hepatotoxicity.
I had a bad rash and my pharmacist told me to stop the amoxicillin immediately. In two days it was completely cleared up — it was definitely the amoxicillin.
Doxycycline was ceased and within 48 hours the patient reported complete resolution of nausea and epigastric pain. Dechallenge positive; alternative antibiotic prescribed.
We stopped co-trimoxazole but the rash did not improve at all — dechallenge was negative. The drug is not the cause and further investigation for an alternative aetiology is warranted.
Despite discontinuing metformin, the diarrhea persisted unchanged for over two weeks. Negative dechallenge suggests the gastrointestinal symptoms are not drug-related.
I stopped the amlodipine tablets but the headache continued just the same for another ten days — it was not from the drug. My doctor is now looking for another cause.
Even after stopping efavirenz for two weeks, the patient's dizziness remained unchanged, suggesting an alternative aetiology such as vestibular disorder. Dechallenge negative.
Isoniazid was held for ten days but the patient continued to experience joint pain without any improvement. Negative dechallenge; arthralgia likely attributable to underlying disease.
We discontinued artemether-lumefantrine but the patient's headache and fatigue persisted for another week. The dechallenge was negative, indicating an alternative explanation for the symptoms.
My mother stopped the chloroquine but her nausea did not get better at all. The nurses said the drug probably was not the cause of her sickness.
Nevirapine was discontinued on suspicion of drug-induced rash; however, the rash progressed and worsened despite withdrawal. Negative dechallenge; viral exanthem suspected instead.
I stopped the tenofovir for three weeks on my own but the fatigue was still there every day. My doctor confirmed the medicine was not causing my tiredness.
Drug held: pyrazinamide. Dechallenge outcome: joint pain persisted without improvement over 14-day observation. Assessment: negative dechallenge; gout from other causes considered.
Despite holding rifampicin for one week, liver enzyme levels remained markedly elevated and the patient continued to complain of liver pain. Dechallenge was negative for rifampicin.
Even after stopping amoxicillin, the patient's stomach cramps persisted daily without relief for two weeks, suggesting an alternative gastrointestinal aetiology. Dechallenge was negative.
I quit the doxycycline completely but the vomiting carried on for another week, just as bad as before. It was not the tablets causing the problem.
Metformin was withheld for two weeks but the patient's nausea did not resolve, remaining at the same severity. Negative dechallenge; gastroparesis from diabetes suspected.
We stopped co-trimoxazole for ten days but the fatigue and headache were completely unchanged. Dechallenge was negative and the drug has been restarted for HIV prophylaxis.
When efavirenz was reintroduced, the dizziness and vivid nightmares recurred within 48 hours — positive rechallenge confirms drug causality. The patient was switched to dolutegravir.
She was rechallenged with co-trimoxazole and the same maculopapular rash appeared again within three days — confirmed ADR. Rechallenge positive; drug permanently discontinued.
I started taking isoniazid again and the nausea came back immediately, just like before. My stomach was hurting the same way it did the first time.
On restarting nevirapine, the patient again developed an extensive rash within 72 hours — rechallenge positive. This confirms nevirapine as the definitive cause of the cutaneous reaction.
Rechallenge with rifampicin was performed cautiously at low dose and liver pain recurred with rising transaminases within one week. Positive rechallenge; definite hepatotoxicity.
When my doctor gave me amoxicillin again to test it, the itching and rash came back within two days, just as bad as the first time. He confirmed it was the drug.
Pyrazinamide was reintroduced at a lower dose for rechallenge purposes; within five days the patient again developed severe joint pain — rechallenge positive, confirming drug-induced arthralgia.
The patient was rechallenged with doxycycline and experienced the same epigastric pain and nausea within 24 hours of resumption. Positive rechallenge confirmed the causal relationship.
I tried taking the metformin again after a month without it and the diarrhea came right back within two days. I am certain now that metformin was causing it all along.
Drug rechallenge: artemether-lumefantrine restarted for a second malaria episode. Outcome: patient developed vomiting and dizziness again within 48 hours. Rechallenge positive; ADR confirmed.
After switching back to tenofovir, the patient's fatigue returned within one week and renal function began to decline again. Positive rechallenge strongly implicates tenofovir nephrotoxicity.
My husband started chloroquine again for a new infection and the same dizziness and headache came back within three days. We know now that it is from the chloroquine.
Amlodipine was reintroduced and within ten days the patient again developed lower limb oedema and headache — positive rechallenge. This establishes a definite drug-adverse effect relationship.

When isoniazid was restarted as part of sequential rechallenge, the patient developed peripheral neuropathy symptoms again within two weeks. Rechallenge was positive; pyridoxine supplementation intensified.
She was placed back on efavirenz after a brief trial off and the neuropsychiatric symptoms reappeared within 48 hours — dizziness, confusion and disturbing dreams. Positive rechallenge confirmed.
Patient was rechallenged with co-trimoxazole — no rash, no discomfort. The previous episode of skin reaction was coincidental and not drug-related.
I took the amoxicillin again and had absolutely no reaction this time. My doctor confirmed that it was not the drug that caused my rash the first time.

Rechallenge with metformin was performed and the patient tolerated it well with no adverse gastrointestinal effects. Negative rechallenge; previous diarrhea was attributed to concurrent gastroenteritis.
Doxycycline was reintroduced and the patient had no nausea or stomach pain on this occasion. Negative rechallenge confirmed that the earlier gastrointestinal symptoms had an alternative cause.
My doctor restarted the amlodipine and this time I had no headache, no swelling — nothing. He said the swelling I had before was from another reason, not the blood pressure pill.

Rechallenge with chloroquine was conducted for a new malaria episode — the patient experienced no dizziness, no vomiting, and no adverse effects whatsoever. Negative rechallenge; prior symptoms were malaria itself.
Drug rechallenge: isoniazid reintroduced at full dose. Outcome: no nausea, no liver pain, and liver function tests remained within normal limits throughout. Negative rechallenge.

Rifampicin was cautiously reintroduced and liver enzymes remained stable over three weeks of monitoring with no symptoms. Negative rechallenge indicates the earlier hepatitis was not rifampicin-induced.
When I went back on tenofovir, I felt completely fine. The tiredness I had before must have been from my illness, not from the medicine, because I had no problems this time.

Rechallenge with nevirapine at reduced dose showed no recurrence of rash or other adverse events over a four-week observation period. Negative rechallenge; original reaction attributed to concurrent infection.
